codeReview.summary: Excellent implementation of 29 integration tests for workspace discovery. Tests comprehensively cover subdirectory discovery (VAL-INIT-004), global fallback (VAL-INIT-005), workspace consistency (VAL-CROSS-004), error recovery (VAL-CROSS-007), ID prefix consistency (VAL-CROSS-009), and first-use experience (VAL-CROSS-010). All tests pass, code is well-structured with clear validation assertion mapping.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Test file uses 6-12 character ID prefixes to avoid ambiguity from time-sorted ULIDs. This is a practical pattern discovered during implementation that could be documented in AGENTS.md for future test authors.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: werk-cli/tests/discovery.rs lines 332, 500, 607 - tests use &tension.id[..6] or [...12] to ensure unique prefix resolution when multiple tensions created rapidly

sharedStateObservations.observation: The note command has different CLI syntax than other commands (text as positional, --id as flag vs ID as positional for other commands). The worker correctly adapted test syntax after initial failures.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker changed from `note <id> 'text'` to `note 'text' --id <id>` after discovering the actual CLI structure. werk-cli/tests/discovery.rs lines 291, 940 show correct usage.

summary: Feature fully implemented with 29 comprehensive integration tests. All validation assertions from the feature description are covered. Tests properly use TempDir for isolation, verify both CLI output and store state, and handle edge cases like deep nesting (12 levels) and time-sorted ULID prefixes. No blocking issues. Two minor shared-state observations noted about test patterns that could benefit future workers.
